LAW AND MOTION 301, DEC-09-2025 RE: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PINE & TAYLOR CORPORATION DEMURS TO PLAINTIFF KEVIN WARSHAW'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (FAC). THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT301TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kiran N. Bhat, Esq., KELLER POSTMAN LLC kiran.bhat@kellerpostman.com 202.741.5220 Attorney for Plaintiff
John Saadeh, Esq., Saadeh Law Firm (415) 418-9234 john@saadehlawfirm.com attonrey for PINE & TAYLOR CORPORATION, D/B/A HAIGHT BOURBON SHOP
Noelle Krawiec, CSR# 14255 noelleckrawiec@gmail.com Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED/discussed. After Discussion/Argument, the Court vacates is tenative ruling and rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PINE & TAYLOR CORPORATION, D/B/A HAIGHT BOURBON SHOP DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is taken off calender, plaintiff did not respond to defendant's effort to meet and confer (CCP 430.41) Defendant's new deadline to respond to the complaint is September 18, 2025. The parties are ordered to comply with the statutory meet and confer obligations. See filed order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
